chief_complaint: A 65-year-old female developed sudden shortness of breath and disturbed consciousness 1 day after hip replacement surgery for a left femoral neck fracture.
history: The patient had a history of trauma and surgery. She was misdiagnosed as having an acute cerebral infarction.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not explicitly stated.
labs: Cranial diffusion-weighted imaging revealed typical changes indicative of the '3-territory sign'; transthoracic echocardiography confirmed the presence of a patent foramen ovale (PFO); and the transcranial Doppler bubble test detected signals of fat embolism.
correct_diagnosis: Cerebral fat embolism, pulmonary fat embolism, and patent foramen ovale (PFO)
differentials: Cerebral fat embolism, pulmonary fat embolism, and patent foramen ovale (PFO)
correct_ranking: Cerebral fat embolism, pulmonary fat embolism, and patent foramen ovale (PFO)
